title: catharanthus trichophyllus
intro: Catharanthus trichophyllus, also known as the pink periwinkle or Madagascar periwinkle, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It is a flowering plant known for its bright pink, trumpet-shaped flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and stimulate the production of white blood cells. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its alkaloid compounds vinblastine and vincristine, which have been used in the treatment of various cancers and have shown potential in reducing inflammation and improving cognitive function. Herbal preparations of Catharanthus trichophyllus can be found in supplement form, as well as in teas and tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and overall health.
